aids.title: Triplet = RCV
aids.content: Each entry is a **R**ow-**C**olumn-**V**alue triple. First entry stores total Rows, Columns, and number of Values (non-zero terms).

aids.title: Multilist Node: 5 Fields
aids.content:
Every non-zero node has 5 fields:
1. row
2. col
3. value
4. next-in-row pointer
5. next-in-col pointer

Each node lives in exactly 2 linked lists (its row list and its column list).

aids.title: Fast Transpose Two-Pass
aids.content:
Pass 1: Count non-zero elements per column.
Pass 2: Compute starting positions, then place elements directly.
Time: O(columns + non-zero terms).

aids.title: When Is a Matrix Sparse?
aids.content: Rule of thumb: if non-zero elements < (rows * cols) / 3, use sparse representation. The crossover point depends on node overhead vs. wasted zero storage.
